title: BeyondTrust Password Safe Credential Rotation Compliance
dataSources: `sourcetype=beyondtrust:vault` scheduled password change, rotation, and verification events
app: Splunk Add-on for BeyondTrust Password Safe
spl:
index=pam sourcetype="beyondtrust:vault" earliest=-60d
| eval evt=lower(coalesce(event_type, EventType, action, Action, ""))
| eval acct=coalesce(account_name, AccountName, system_account, "")
| eval sys=coalesce(system_name, SystemName, asset, "")
| where match(evt, "(?i)rotate|change.password|reconcile|verification")
| stats latest(_time) as last_rotation by acct sys
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_rotation)/86400,1)
| lookup pam_rotation_sla.csv system_tier OUTPUT max_days
| eval max_days=coalesce(max_days,90)
| where days_since>max_days
| sort -days_since
description: Stale shared credentials violate SOX, PCI, and cyber-insurance expectations. Comparing last successful rotation timestamps from Password Safe to per-system SLAs surfaces accounts that bypass automated rotation jobs.
value: Prevents credential-sprawl incidents and gives GRC teams defensible metrics on vault hygiene across server tiers.
implementation: (1) Build `pam_rotation_sla.csv` mapping system groups to max age. (2) Ensure rotation success events are distinct from failed attempts. (3) Exclude break-glass accounts with manual evidence workflow. (4) Auto-ticket owners when overdue. (5) Review quarterly with IAM.
visualization: Table (account, system, days since rotation, SLA), bar chart (overdue by tier), single-value (percent compliant).
grandmaExplanation: We use identity and sign-in data in Splunk so we can notice unusual logins, access changes, and privileged use while it still matters — BeyondTrust Password Safe Credential Rotation Compliance
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw directory, IdP, PAM, or device feeds.
knownFalsePositives: Routine privileged access from approved change windows, scheduled maintenance, ITSM-tied tickets, or break-glass exercises documented in your runbook.
